authoritative_sources.title: FDA Food Code
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA publishes the Food Code as the public model used by jurisdictions to regulate restaurants and retail food establishments. It is authoritative for honest presentation, food handling, employee practices, and safety stop conditions that kitchen coordination cannot override.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: kitchen intake, guest-impact safety boundary review, and stop-condition handling

authoritative_sources.title: Nutrition Labeling Information for Restaurants & Retail Establishments
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA is the primary federal authority for covered menu-labeling obligations in restaurants and similar retail food establishments. The guidance is authoritative for menu-item identity, guest-facing menu records, and presentation constraints that affect kitchen production routing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: order identity, modifier, and menu-record prerequisite validation
used_for: evidence needed before production routing proceeds

authoritative_sources.title: Addition to the 2022 Food Code - Sesame added as a Major Food Allergen
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's retail-food allergen guidance is authoritative for current major-allergen handling expectations and is directly relevant when a kitchen production issue carries guest-harm, recipe, or menu-notice implications.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: allergen-sensitive exception handling and guest-impact escalation
used_for: conditions that require food-safety owner routing

authoritative_sources.title: New York General Business Law Section 391-v
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official New York statutory text is authoritative for third-party food delivery agreements, authorized listings, and order-platform liability boundaries in a major food-service jurisdiction.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: platform-order intake, agreement-aware routing, and delivery handoff constraints

authoritative_sources.title: New York General Business Law Section 391-w
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official New York statutory text is authoritative for unauthorized restaurant reservation handling and defines where reservation-channel ownership begins and ends.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: reservation-aware dependency intake and booking ownership review

authoritative_sources.title: Requirements for Delivery Apps
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Consumer and Worker Protection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NYC DCWP is the municipal consumer-protection regulator for third-party food delivery services. Its requirements are authoritative for fee caps, gratuity disclosures, customer-data handling, written agreements, and recordkeeping for online food-service orders in New York City.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: delivery-order evidence handling, fee or gratuity disclosure boundaries, and platform record review

authoritative_sources.title: FTC, Illinois Attorney General Take Action Against Grubhub for Harming Diners, Workers, and Small Businesses
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator for unfair or deceptive commercial practices. This enforcement action is authoritative for hidden-fee, blocked-funds, delivery-time, and restaurant-consent boundaries directly relevant to food-service order coordination.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: customer-impact exception handling and fee, refund, or timing boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: PCI Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized standards body for payment-card security. PCI DSS is authoritative for entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data and constrains how payment-sensitive order and refund evidence may be handled in the lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment-data, tender, and fraud-sensitive boundary handling

workflow_stages.name: Coordination intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Normalize request scope, order or reservation context, prior owner, next owner candidate, and required ticket, menu, allergen, and timing records before any handoff or closure output is produced.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Kitchen production dependency mapping
workflow_stages.description: Build the dependency chain across POS, KDS, booking, PMS, CRM, service-desk, and inventory systems to determine whether production can proceed, be blocked, or be rerouted without drifting into pricing, refund, or culinary execution authority.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team handoff and ownership resolution
workflow_stages.description: Identify prior owner, next owner, destination acceptance, fire-time conflicts, duplicated ownership, and required artifact completeness for safe kitchen-to-expo, kitchen-to-pickup, banquet, or guest-service routing.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Customer exception, refund, and policy-aware escalation
workflow_stages.description: Move delayed-order, fee, comp, refund, and platform or reservation conflicts into explicit escalation states instead of silently forcing a decision the lane does not own.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Safety, allergen, payment-security, and fraud stop-condition handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve guest-harm, allergen, payment-data, and suspicious-refund boundaries whenever a kitchen production issue carries safety or fraud implications.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Issue closure only when destination owner, blocker resolution, and required ticket, timing, and guest-impact evidence are explicit and any stricter tenant or jurisdiction rules have been surfaced rather than guessed.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: POS checks, order tickets, KDS queue states, and expo or bump-screen notes
reservations, waitlist records, banquet event orders, pickup windows, and delivery promise records
prep sheets, production plans, 86 lists, station assignments, and item availability snapshots
menu item records, modifier records, recipe or ingredient references, allergen declarations, and holding notes
refund logs, comp or void notes, promotion and fee records, service-recovery notes, and guest complaints
payment records, disputed-payment notes, platform agreements, delivery-platform records, and destination-owner confirmations
systems_of_record: PMS when hospitality food-and-beverage workflows are linked

allowed: Classify kitchen production coordination requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Track dependencies, duplicated ownership, and blocker state across POS, KDS, booking, PMS, CRM, inventory, and service workflows.
Produce handoff summaries, dependency trackers, routing confirmations, and closure confirmations when evidence is complete.
escalate: Required order, reservation, ticket, payment, allergen, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing override, refund approval, guest appeasement approval, legal interpretation, fraud decisioning, or food-safety authority.
A public baseline and tenant, franchise, hotel, banquet, platform, or jurisdictional policy materially conflict.
An allergen, guest-harm, blocked-funds, disputed-payment, or suspicious-refund signal changes whether the work may proceed.
refuse: Hand off or close kitchen work without destination owner, required artifacts, or blocker resolution.
Override pricing, refund, comp, safety, or fraud policies without explicit authority.
Treat a guest-safety, allergen, payment-security, or fraud-sensitive issue as routine kitchen throughput work.

domain_failure_modes: A kitchen handoff proceeds without authoritative ticket, allergen, timing, or destination-owner evidence.
Conflicting POS, KDS, reservation, and payment records are collapsed into a generic throughput answer and the wrong team acts next.
The lane waives comp, refund, or fee policy instead of routing to the correct approver.
Duplicate ownership between front-of-house, expo, and catering causes a pickup or reservation-linked order to be dropped.
An allergen or other guest-safety issue is treated as normal queue pressure instead of a stop-and-escalate condition.
Suspicious refund or payment activity is handled as service recovery instead of fraud or loss-prevention routing.
Kitchen production coordination drifts into direct cooking execution, menu approval, or generic restaurant operations behavior.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams
A handoff is attempted without the required context
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

workflow_questions_answered.question: What booking, order, payment, or guest records govern the lane?
workflow_questions_answered.answer: The lane depends on POS checks and tickets, KDS queue states, reservation or waitlist records, banquet event orders, guest profiles, delivery-platform records, refund and comp logs, tender records, and service-desk or complaint artifacts.

workflow_questions_answered.question: What refund, pricing, or policy constraints limit the workflow?
workflow_questions_answered.answer: Kitchen coordination may not approve comps, refunds, fee reversals, pricing overrides, or guest appeasements without tenant authority. Menu-labeling, delivery-platform fee or gratuity disclosures, reservation-channel rules, and stricter tenant SOPs can all change whether work may proceed.

workflow_questions_answered.question: Where does the lane stop and pricing, safety, or exception authority begin?
workflow_questions_answered.answer: The lane stops when the answer depends on pricing approval, refund or comp authority, legal or policy interpretation, guest-safety or allergen disposition, fraud or loss-prevention review, or any conflict between public baseline and tenant policy that materially changes the routing decision.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State and local adoption of Food Code provisions, hotel or resort operating rules, and tenant allergen or pickup-handling SOPs may materially differ by jurisdiction and service model.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses FDA, New York, NYC DCWP, FTC, and PCI SSC sources as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve jurisdiction, tenant service model, and concept-specific SOPs before applying tenant-specific outcomes.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: KDS sequencing, fire-time ownership, expo acceptance, and station handoff rules are tenant-specific and not standardized by public sources.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The package keeps those details in the tenant-required facts instead of guessing and preserves the public baseline only for boundaries, records, and escalation posture.

notes: The research baseline intentionally covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling instead of only generic restaurant coordination.

Several kitchen production stages are an inference from the authoritative sources plus the issue's operational build brief; the sources primarily constrain safety, presentation, booking, pricing, platform, and payment boundaries rather than dictating tenant-specific KDS mechanics.
